text.title: LB Monitoring Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in LB monitoring deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Pager fires every minute during a transient 1-pod GC pause; SRE muts alerts; a real outage rings 4 hours later, ignored.
antipattern.root-cause: Alert rules use `for: 30s` or omit `for:` entirely; transient blips immediately page.
antipattern.fix: Set `for:` per alert (BackendDown 1 m, HighErrorRate 5 m, HighLatency 5 m, PoolExhausted 2 m). Re-tune only if SLO needs faster reaction; never go below 1 m.

antipattern.symptom: Alert says "5xx rate high" with no `backend` label. SRE spends 20 minutes hunting which backend pool is failing.
antipattern.root-cause: Recording rule aggregated away the `backend` dimension to "simplify" the metric.
antipattern.fix: Preserve the `backend` label in every metric + alert. Re-aggregate inside the dashboard, never at metric ingest.

antipattern.symptom: Investigating last night's incident requires SSH into the LB box; the host had crashed and was reprovisioned, logs are gone forever.
antipattern.root-cause: No log shipping configured; logs lived on local disk only.
antipattern.fix: Ship access logs via Promtail / Filebeat to Loki / ELK with 30-day retention. Tail the LB stdout / syslog; parse access-log format into structured fields.
